entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) studied for yield and rotation effects

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Vigna unguiculata) studied for yield and rotation effects

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop (Arachis hypogaea) studied for yield and rotation effects

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the crops measured in the study

entities.name: SOIL CHEMICAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The chemical characteristics of the soil including pH, carbon, and nitrogen

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: The measure of acidity or alkalinity of the soil

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The amount of carbon found in the soil organic matter

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil

entities.name: EXCHANGEABLE BASES
entities.description: The cations that can be exchanged on soil particles

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The decomposed organic material in the soil

entities.name: MINERAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The inorganic nitrogen available for plant uptake

entities.name: NITROGEN AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The extent to which nitrogen is available for plant use in the soil

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: The application of nitrogen-based nutrients to the soil to increase productivity

entities.name: CONTINUOUS FALLOW
entities.description: The practice of leaving the land unplanted for a continuous period

entities.name: MONOCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop year after year on the same land

entities.name: SANDY SOIL
entities.description: A soil type characterized by a high proportion of sand particles

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region characterized by low and irregular rainfall

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country in West Africa where the research sites were located

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The region where the study was conducted

relationships.description: Significantly increased the yield of pearl millet, cowpea, and groundnut
Resulted in lower yields compared to legume-millet rotations
Affects the chemical properties of the soil
Supplied more mineral nitrogen than legume-millet rotations
Cowpea-millet rotation provided greater nitrogen availability than continuous millet
Yield was measured for this crop

Yield was measured for this crop
The study was conducted in sandy soil within Niger
Niger is located in West Africa
Did not differ from monoculture in maintaining soil organic matter
